questions.question: Describe the appearance of the statue of the Happy Prince.
questions.answer: The statue of the Happy Prince was very beautiful. It was covered with thin leaves of fine gold. Its eyes were made of bright sapphires and a large red ruby shone on the sword. The statue stood high above the city on a tall column.

questions.question: Why did the swallow stop in the city?
questions.answer: The swallow stopped in the city while travelling to Egypt. He was looking for a place to rest for the night. He chose the statue of the Happy Prince as his resting place because it was high and had fresh air.

questions.question: Why was the Happy Prince unhappy?
questions.answer: The Happy Prince was unhappy because he could see the suffering and poverty in the city. When he was alive, he lived happily in a palace and did not know sorrow. After becoming a statue, he realised how many people were suffering.

questions.question: Explain how the prince helped the seamstress.
questions.answer: The prince saw a poor seamstress whose child was sick. He asked the swallow to take the ruby from his sword and give it to her. The swallow carried the ruby and left it on the table near the woman.

questions.question: Describe how the swallow helped the sick child.
questions.answer: The swallow gently flew around the room and fanned the child’s forehead with his wings. The cool breeze helped the boy feel better and he soon fell asleep peacefully.

questions.question: Why did the prince send a sapphire to the playwright?
questions.answer: The prince sent a sapphire to a poor playwright who was trying to finish a play but was too cold and hungry to write. The jewel helped him sell it and buy food and firewood.

questions.question: Why did the prince give his second eye to the match girl?
questions.answer: The match girl had dropped her matches in the gutter and had no money to take home. The prince asked the swallow to give his second sapphire eye to her so she could sell it.

questions.question: Why did the swallow refuse to go to Egypt?
questions.answer: The swallow refused to go to Egypt because he loved the prince and wanted to help him. He decided to stay with the prince and assist him in helping the poor.

questions.question: How did the prince help the poor people of the city?
questions.answer: The prince asked the swallow to remove the gold leaves from his body and give them to the poor people. The gold helped them buy food and survive.

questions.question: Describe what the swallow saw when he flew over the city.
questions.answer: The swallow saw rich people enjoying themselves in beautiful houses while poor people were suffering from hunger and poverty.

questions.question: Why did the swallow become weak in winter?
questions.answer: The swallow became weak because winter arrived and the weather was extremely cold. He also had very little food to eat.

questions.question: Describe the death of the swallow.
questions.answer: The swallow became very weak due to the cold. Before dying, he kissed the prince on the lips and then fell dead at the prince’s feet.

questions.question: What happened to the prince’s heart after the swallow died?
questions.answer: When the swallow died, the prince’s lead heart broke into two pieces due to sadness.

questions.question: Why did the Mayor order the statue to be removed?
questions.answer: The Mayor believed the statue had become ugly because it had lost its gold and jewels. Therefore he ordered it to be pulled down.

questions.question: What happened to the statue after it was removed?
questions.answer: The statue was melted in a furnace by the workers.

questions.question: Why did the lead heart not melt in the furnace?
questions.answer: The lead heart did not melt because it was very hard and broken.

questions.question: Why were the prince and swallow considered precious?
questions.answer: They were considered precious because they showed kindness, compassion, and sacrifice for others.

questions.question: What did the angel bring to God?
questions.answer: The angel brought the lead heart of the prince and the dead swallow.

questions.question: Why did God call them the most precious things?
questions.answer: God called them precious because of their kindness and selfless sacrifice.

questions.question: Explain the friendship between the prince and the swallow.
questions.answer: The prince and the swallow develop a strong bond. The swallow helps the prince give away his riches to the poor and stays with him even during winter.

questions.question: What moral lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches that kindness and sacrifice are more valuable than wealth and beauty.

questions.question: How does the prince help people even after death?
questions.answer: As a statue, the prince asks the swallow to distribute his jewels and gold to the poor.

questions.question: What contrast does the story show in the city?
questions.answer: The story shows a contrast between rich people living comfortably and poor people suffering from hunger and poverty.

questions.question: How does the swallow show loyalty?
questions.answer: The swallow shows loyalty by staying with the prince and helping him even when winter arrives.

questions.question: Summarise the story 'The Happy Prince'.
questions.answer: The story tells how the statue of the Happy Prince and a swallow work together to help the poor people of the city. The prince sacrifices his jewels and gold, and the swallow sacrifices his life. Their kindness is finally recognised by God.
